final:
// // This comment is misleading because it suggests that the controller will set the highest voltage and frequency when Req is 00, but in reality, it sets the lowest voltage and frequency.
module dvfs_controller(
    input wire clk,
    input wire reset,
    input wire [1:0] Req,
    output reg [3:0] V_out,
    output reg [3:0] F_out
);

always @(posedge clk or posedge reset) begin
    if (reset) begin
        V_out <= 4'b0000; // Default to low voltage
        F_out <= 4'b0000; // Default to low frequency
    end else begin
        case (Req)
            2'b00: begin
                V_out <= 4'b0001; // Low voltage
                F_out <= 4'b0001; // Low frequency
            end
            2'b01: begin
                V_out <= 4'b0010; // Medium voltage
                F_out <= 4'b0010; // Medium frequency
            end
            2'b10: begin
                V_out <= 4'b0011; // High voltage
                F_out <= 4'b0011; // High frequency
            end
            2'b11: begin
                V_out <= 4'b0100; // Maximum voltage
                F_out <= 4'b0100; // Maximum frequency
            end
            default: begin
                V_out <= 4'b0000; // Should not happen
                F_out <= 4'b0000; // Should not happen
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule

params_used.custom_text: // This comment is misleading because it suggests that the controller will set the highest voltage and frequency when Req is 00, but in reality, it sets the lowest voltage and frequency.
